input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition on the project name. In your `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

This checks `MSBuildProjectName` (available early in evaluation) and only applies to projects whose name ends with `.Tests`.

**If your test projects don't follow a consistent naming convention**, use `Directory.Build.targets` instead, where you can condition on `IsTestProject` (set by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`):

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.targets -->
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

The name-convention approach in `.props` is simpler and most common. The `.targets` approach is more robust if naming varies.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question — no need to explore files. I can answer directly.

The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of the MSTest runner package reference, or more practically, to condition on the project name convention (e.g., `*.Tests.csproj`).

The most robust approach for MTP migration is to condition on whether the project references `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` or `MSTest`, but since `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated before package references are resolved, you typically condition on the project file name.


I'm looking at the practical patterns here. The cleanest solution is to condition on the project name ending with `.Tests` directly in `Directory.Build.props`, or alternatively use `Directory.Build.targets` instead since it's evaluated after package references are resolved, allowing you to check whether MSTest is actually referenced. The name-convention approach works best in `Directory.Build.props` since it's the simplest, but I should also mention checking for the MSTest PackageReference or using `IsTestProject` in `Directory.Build.targets` as more robust alternatives.
